title: B.C.R.R. Co. Zone Check
desc: Zone check issued by B.C.R.R. Co. for surrender at the city line.
notes: Listed as NY997A in the Zone Checks section of the Atwood-Coffee catalogue.
wantedNote: Actively seeking an example of this token.

title: Motor Bus Society 1948-1998 Fifty Years of Preserving History
obv: MOTOR BUS SOCIETY 1948-1998 FIFTY YEARS OF PRESERVING HISTORY (OLD DOUBLE-DECK BUS WITH 50-MBS ON FRONT) YELLOW COACH MODEL Z
rev: Good For One Tour New York, NY 50th Anniversary April 17-19, 1998
desc: Club tour token issued for the Motor Bus Society 50th Anniversary events in New York City.
notes: Brass example listed as New York CT (2), number 1. Contemporary Farebox commentary described these as thick, heavy, proof-like pieces and among the most attractive tour tokens produced. Additional off-catalog examples were reportedly struck in other metals, but only the tour tokens actually used and readily available were cataloged. Offered to collectors in 1998 for $3 plus $1 postage and handling.
usage: Used for the Saturday night sightseeing tour.

sources.title: Farebox
sources.note: Announcement notes that NY CT-1 and CT-2 were struck by Masterpiece Medallions and that other off-catalog metals exist.

sources.title: Farebox
sources.note: Notes usage, maker location, and original collector offering price.

title: Motor Bus Society 1948-1998 Fifty Years of Preserving History
obv: MOTOR BUS SOCIETY 1948-1998 FIFTY YEARS OF PRESERVING HISTORY (OLD DOUBLE-DECK BUS WITH 50-MBS ON FRONT) YELLOW COACH MODEL Z
rev: Good For One Tour New York, NY 50th Anniversary April 17-19, 1998
desc: Club tour token issued for the Motor Bus Society 50th Anniversary events in New York City.
notes: White metal example listed as New York CT (2), number 2. Contemporary Farebox commentary described these as thick, heavy, proof-like pieces and among the most attractive tour tokens produced. Additional off-catalog examples were reportedly struck in other metals, but only the tour tokens actually used and readily available were cataloged. Offered to collectors in 1998 for $5 plus $1 postage and handling.

title: Fulton Ferry Crossing E-River
obv: FULTON FERRY CROSSING E-RIVER / BROOKLYN TO MANHATTAN
rev: Four Cents (ferryboat) Nassau / Admit the Bearer (all incuse)
desc: Fantasy token copying an authentic Fulton Ferry ticket design for a Brooklyn to Manhattan crossing.
notes: Listed as FF-NY-1 in the Atwood-Coffee catalogue. Manufactured in the 1970s and wholesaled to dealers for a few cents apiece. The design is copied after an authentic ticket issued by the Fulton Ferry.
wantedNote: Actively seeking an example of this token.

title: NYCTA Bombardier R62A Inaugural Service Run
obv: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT AUTHORITY / BOMBARDIER (LOGO)
rev: R62A Good For One Ride / Inaugural Service Run
desc: Fantasy token referencing the introduction of R62A subway cars by the New York City Transit Authority.
notes: Listed as FF-NY-2 in the Atwood-Coffee catalogue. Associated with the Bombardier-built R62A cars and their inaugural service.

title: NY 630 AY Presentation Piece
rev: H STERN incused / numeral engraved on edge
desc: Presentation piece based on NY 630 AY, struck in 14k gold with edge numeral and H STERN incused.
notes: Listed as PP-AM in the Presentation Pieces section of the Atwood-Coffee catalogue. Struck in 14k gold. The catalogue notes that 500 were made.

title: Department of Marine Aviation / LaGuardia Field
obv: DEPT. OF MARINE AVIATION / LAGUARDIA FIELD
notes: Listed in the Atwood-Coffee Transportation Related category as New York - New York City: Queens NY515A. Details in Farebox: March, 2020.

title: New-York and Albany Peoples Line of Steam Boats
obv: NEW-YORK AND ALBANY PEOPLES LINE OF STEAM BOATS
rev: Time Table Leave N.Y. 6 P.M. / Leave Albany 7 ½ P.M.
desc: Timetable token for the New-York and Albany Peoples Line of Steam Boats.
notes: Listed as TT-G in the timetable token section. Bronze, 19mm, struck round. Catalog value 15.00.

title: Shore Line Boston to New York
obv: SHORE LINE BOSTON TO NEW YORK
rev: Leave Boston at 1 P.M. & 9:30 P.M. / Sundays at 9:30 P.M.
desc: Timetable token for the Shore Line route between Boston and New York.
notes: Listed as TT-H in the timetable token section. Vulcanite, 32mm, struck round. Catalog value 45.00.

title: Stonington Line Boston to New York
desc: Timetable token for the Stonington Line route between Boston and New York.
notes: Listed as TT-I in the timetable token section. Vulcanite, 32mm, struck round. Catalog value 125.00.

title: Stonington Line Boston to New York
obv: STONINGTON LINE BOSTON TO NEW YORK
rev: Leave Boston at 6 P.M. Except Sundays
desc: Timetable token for the Stonington Line route between Boston and New York.
notes: Listed as TT-J in the timetable token section. Vulcanite, 32mm, struck round. Catalog value 60.00.
